your_book:
July 2 was a broad lift for Jaleel's book, with the account sitting at $20,697.98 and an unrealized gain of $158.01 (+0.77%). The day's standout movers were BSX (+4.83%), IOT (+5.12%), VRTX (+6.03%), ADBE (+4.14%), and GILD (+4.21%) — a wide sweep across healthcare and tech that did most of the heavy lifting. ABT (+3.49%) and ZTS (+3.24%) added further. The lone red was NVDA (-1.39%), modest in dollar terms at ~$1,271 but worth noting given export-control headwinds flagged in the risk log. Cash is effectively zero ($2.49), so the book has no dry powder to deploy or buffer volatility.

The drift problem is concentrated in three names where price and thesis have separated. ADBE is the largest holding at $3,442 (16.6% of the book) yet sits at a -10.4% loss — today's +4.14% bounce is welcome, but it hasn't closed that gap, and the risk flag is clear: the narrative (AI-moat fear, CFO exit) is overriding strong fundamentals. BSX (+4.83% today, -5.7% overall) popped hard on a day when its FY26 growth guide has already been slashed from 10.5% to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat through Q3 — that one-day move deserves skepticism, not celebration. LULU (-7.4% overall, five straight quarters of negative Americas comps) continues to erode quietly at $1,066.

Watch the July earnings cluster closely: NOW and BSX both report around July 23, and ABT on July 17 — three of the four largest holdings will have refreshed numbers within three weeks. IOT and RKLB remain rated AVOID by the model (QARP scores of 48.5 and 37.0 respectively), and together represent ~8.9% of the book; at a capital-preservation mandate, holding two AVOID-rated names without a documented counter-thesis is a standing question. The KO options sweep on the Jul 17 $83 calls ($0.941 vs. 362 OI, 2,061 contracts) is a flagged market signal ahead of KO earnings on July 28.

attention.headline: Largest holding, -10.4% underwater — bounce doesn't fix the thesis gap
attention.note: At 16.6% of the book and a $3,442 position still in the red, today's +4.14% is noise against a persistent narrative overhang (AI-moat fear, CFO exit); next catalyst isn't until ~Sep 11, so the question is whether this weight is justified while the loss compounds.

attention.headline: +4.83% day pop sits on top of a slashed guide and flat Q3 outlook
attention.note: FY26 growth cut from 10.5% to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat through Q3; a strong single-day move before the ~Jul 23 earnings print warrants scrutiny — the risk flag is elevated, not resolved.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, -4.8% in the hole, near-zero QARP (37.0) at a capital-preservation mandate
attention.note: RKLB carries the book's lowest quality score and an AVOID verdict; at $1,306 (6.3% weight) with no visible catalyst, holding it conflicts directly with the portfolio's stated objective unless there is a documented speculative carve-out.

attention.headline: Five straight quarters of negative Americas comps — guidance cut and -7.4% loss with no floor visible
attention.note: Tariff margin pressure, stalled China, and a FY guide cut make this the book's clearest fundamental deterioration story; next print is ~Sep 4, which is a long time to sit in a -7.4% position with an elevated risk flag and no catalyst to reverse the trend.
